verStmt.version: v1: Edited, anonymised dataset shared under a CC-BY attribution-only license

stdyInfo.abstract: The Gauteng City-Region Observatory (GCRO) was established in 2008 as a partnership between the University of Johannesburg (UJ), the University of the Witwatersrand (Wits) and the Gauteng Provincial Government (GPG), with municipalities in Gauteng also represented. The objective of the GCRO is to inform policy in various spheres of the Gauteng government with data on the quality of the lives of people who live in Gauteng. The Quality of Life Survey III was conducted in 2013 and 2014.

sumDscr.geogCover: The survey covers the Gauteng province in South Africa
sumDscr.geogUnit: The lowest level of geographic aggregation in the data is ward
sumDscr.universe: The survey covers all adult residents in Gauteng, South Africa

stdyInfo.notes: The GCRO's Quality of Life Survey collects data on demographic details of the enumerated population (population group, gender, age, language) and on housing (dwelling type, tenure, satisfaction with dwelling, perceived quality of housing and housing allocation) as well as household services (water, sanitation, refuse, energy sources). Data was also collected on migration, health (including disability), education and employment (including employment sector). Data on community services and amenities was also collected, and on transport, leisure activities and safety and crime. Financial data was collected (including on debts, income, and social grants) and data on household assets. Data on public participation and governance was also collected during the survey. Finally, the survey collected data on the perceived personal wellbeing and quality of life of respondents.

useStmt.citReq: Gauteng City-Region Observatory (GCRO). Quality of Life Survey 2013-2014 [dataset]. Round 3. Version 1. Johannesburg: GCRO [producer], 2016. Cape Town: DataFirst [distributor], 2016. DOI: https://doi.org/10.25828/gn3g-vc93
useStmt.conditions: Public access data for use under a Creative Commons CC-BY (Attribution-only) License

var.name: recordid
var.labl: Unique identifier

var.name: Munic
var.labl: Municipality

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: Ward
var.labl: Ward

var.name: GCROweight
var.labl: GCRO weight

var.name: D_1
var.labl: Dwellings

var.name: D_3
var.labl: Households

var.name: E_1
var.labl: Eligible respondents

var.name: H_1
var.labl: Population group

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: H_2
var.labl: Sex

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: H_3
var.labl: Dwelling type

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
House, brick or concrete structure on a separate stand
Traditional dwelling, hut or structure made of traditional materials
Flat or apartment in a block of flats
Semi -detached house not in a complex
House, flat or room separate from main dwelling in backyard
Informal dwelling or shack in backyard
Informal dwelling NOT in backyard, e.g. in informal squatter settlement or on a farm
Room or flat which is part of main dwelling or property
Unit in a retirement home or barracks etc.

var.name: H_3_RECODED
var.labl: Dwelling type (recode)

var.name: X1_1
var.labl: Number of rooms

var.name: X1_2
var.labl: Single room shared with other households

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: X1_3
var.labl: Dwelling ownership

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Owned, but paying off a bond
Used government housing subsidy and own contribution
Transfer of title deed of existing government house
Informal dwelling or shack, paying rent
Informal dwelling or shack, not paying rent
The dwelling is on tribal or communal land
House sitting (looking after the house for another owner)
Rent free (allowed to stay rent free by the owner)

var.name: X1_4
var.labl: Main water source

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Piped - in dwelling with no meter
Piped - in dwelling with prepaid meter
Piped - in dwelling with standard meter
Piped - yard tap with no meter
Piped - yard tap with prepaid meter
Street taps or stand pipes, free water
Street taps or stand pipes paid for

var.name: X1_5
var.labl: Is water source more than 200m away?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: X1_9
var.labl: Type of toilet facility

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

Basic pit latrine - pit toilet without ventilation

var.name: X1_10
var.labl: Number of other households sharing toilet

var.name: X1_11
var.labl: How is rubbish disposed?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Refuse removed from the house at least once a week
Refuse removed from the house less often
Thrown in the street or veldt
No refuse removal service at all

var.name: X1_12
var.labl: Number of refuse bags

var.name: X6_1
var.labl: Are you registered to vote in the 2014 elections?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: X6_2
var.labl: Are you planning to vote?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: X6_3
var.labl: If not, why not vote?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Not allowed to vote, for example not RSA citizen
Don't know who to vote for
Does not think his / her vote will make any difference
Don't like politics, broken promises, waste of time

var.name: X6_3_1_RECODED
var.labl: Specify why not vote if 'other' was chosen

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
No response due to skip scenario
Can't stand in queues for too long.
Do not want to waste my time
Don't have a reason to vote
Don't see the need to vote, why is it important for him to vote for them.
Government is treating us in a bad way
He doesn't see any reason for voting because whenever he needs help he never receives any yet they say they are working
He has been voting but still does not have a job, politicians become rich while people continue to be poor.
He wants his title deed before he can vote
He will see if he is available to vote
I am not sure whether I'm gonna vote or not
I did not have time to register
I will see if I will vote
No change the government has got empty promises, children don't have recreational parks & crime is still a problem
Not satisfied with how the government treats people like us.
Respondent is just not sure if she will be at work or have time to vote
Respondent is not sure if she is going to vote or not.
She is still going to think ,and she is voting no jobs
She left her ID at home in North West
Still thinking about it, not sure
The government does want to build her a RDP house
They don't provide anything after voting
Will see if she votes or not

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Sell food (fresh, cooked food or groceries)
Sell clothes, accessories, cosmetics, other goods
Provide services to people (hair salon, barber, tailor, shoe mending)
Provide other services (e.g. panel beating, mechanics, plumbing, building, electrical, security)

Never tried to start a business
My business was a success and I sold it or I stopped running it

var.name: X9_5
var.labl: Why do you buy goods and services from informal sector?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
I know who I am buying from
I can get good brands at good prices

var.name: X9_6
var.labl: Why do you not buy services from informal sector?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Not enough choice or stock too limited
People think I can't afford real shops

var.name: X11_1
var.labl: Where do you usually go for medical care?

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Not applicable, don't usually need health care

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
No public health care facilities available

var.name: X11_3

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario

var.name: X12_1
var.labl: Highest level of education completed

catgry.labl: No response due to skip scenario
Grade 8, Std 6, L6 or Form I
Grade 9, Std 7, L7 or Form II
Grade 10, Std 8, L8, Form III, NTC 1, or RCE higher
A certificate from a college, technikon or university
A diploma from a college, technikon or university
Post graduate degree - e.g. Hons, MA, PhD
